name: Avon City Ford
description:
Avon City Ford commenced trading in 1965 from the current five-acre site opposite the southern roundabout on Blenheim Road, the main arterial route south out of Christchurch. Since then, it has developed into one of the major dealerships in the South Island selling Ford passenger and light commercial vehicles. Avon City Mahindra operates from the same site with the Mahindra franchise.

 

With over 100 staff on site plus a satellite service centre in Rangiora, Avon City Ford covers all motoring needs with expert salespeople, a finance department, car and light commercial technicians, parts advisors, a full paint & panel division and a grooming department. All new vehicles are prepared on site where they are put through a comprehensive pre-delivery check and road test and any accessories are fitted according to the client’s instructions. The grooming department completes the vehicles to ensure they are delivered in pristine condition. Used vehicles are fully checked mechanically prior to going through the paint and panel department to bring them up to our high standard for resale.

 

Our in-house buying team only retain the best possible vehicles for resale. Our finance department offers competitive rates on lease agreements and can organize finance through several vehicle-focused providers by tailoring finance packages to suit all individual needs. We also have a full Auto Training department with a number of course spanning multiple NCEA levels and qualifications as well as a number of industry certified courses.

name: AWOL
description:
New Zealand’s first Adventure Utility Vehicle.
For those ready to take the road less followed.

 

AWOL is a small team in New Zealand building Adventure Utility Vehicles for people who choose the road less followed.

Born from frustration with what existed — and belief in what’s possible — we’re here to make bold, useful machines that enable lives of movement, meaning, and connection.

name: Back To The Wild
description:
Our Whānau and the story behind Back to the Wild

 

As a mum who spends most of her time tramping, skiing, and exploring with her kids, I needed sunscreen and other outdoor skincare that could handle New Zealand’s wild conditions - without the chemical nasties I didn’t want on our skin.

 

With a science degree and a love of DIY, I started formulating my own natural sunscreen just for our family. I made an extra batch for friends, and listed the leftovers on Facebook Marketplace - they sold out almost instantly!

 

This was when I realised I wasn’t the only one searching for something better, and Back to the Wild was born that night back in 2017!

 

Today, Back to the Wild offers a range of small-batch, handcrafted outdoor skincare that’s both effective and gentle on the earth. Every product is made with science-backed ingredients, independently SPF-tested, and packaged sustainably. We’re here to support families who live life outdoors - keeping skin safe, nourished, and ready for adventure.

 

Plus, we've expanded our range to include exclusive tramping books, getting kids excited to head out overnight tramping, and a range of curated tested by us outdoor gear!

 

And if you're into tips for living self-sufficiently, especially on a regular urban block, check out my personal @UrbanSelfSufficiencyNZ blog.

name: Bacon Brothers
description: Bacon Brothers is a beloved New Zealand food brand that started as a humble bacon butty stall at the Christchurch Farmers Market in 2012 — and has since grown into a local institution. Known for their legendary burgers, cheeky banter, and commitment to fresh, locally sourced ingredients, they serve up good vibes alongside great food. With popular locations at Little High Eatery and Riverside Market, as well as a full-service catering operation, Bacon Brothers brings flavour and fun to events across the South Island. Their own craft beer label, BEERS, rounds out the experience, making every visit unforgettable. Passionate about people, quality, and community, Bacon Brothers is more than just a burger joint — it’s a celebration of local goodness.

name: Bader

name: Bays Boating
description:
Bays Boating was started in 2004 by Dave Thorn in his parent's hop shed in the Motueka Valley. It has since grown to be the region’s largest marine retail and service centre, now employing over ten staff. New Zealand’s best boating brands for sale in Motueka Bays Boating works with some of the top brands in New Zealand boating and water sports including Stabicraft, McLay, Fi-Glass, Haines Hunter, Honda and Mercury.

 

Our dedication to the brands has seen Bays Boating awarded McLay Dealer of the Year, two years in a row, and most recently, the first South Island dealership to become a Mercury Premier Service Centre. Offering a state-of-the-art marine repair workshop & service centre In 2019, Bays Boating opened a spacious new showroom and purpose-built workshop where there are five mechanics on-hand to service and repair your boat. In 2016, Bays Boating became part of Boating & Outdoors in New Zealand; the largest marine buying group, so we can offer a huge range of boating accessories at great prices.

name: BEERS by Bacon Bros
description:
Welcome to BEERS by Bacon Bros

We’ve combined our passion for BBQ and BEERS in an old abandoned warehouse.

 

I bargained my life with a bear.

 

The BEERS logo is inspired by a black Hokkaido bear that walked into my campsite in Japan… 

I made a deal with it: “if you don’t eat me, I’ll open up a BBQ Joint in your honour”.
He hasn’t hunted me down and eaten me yet…

 

Our BBQ is inspired by the legends around the world that have passed on their wisdom, from one generation to another, and then on to me.

 

Here at Beer’s, we cook with fire and passion, and as part of that passion we only use the best ingredients from around NZ and the world.

Dare to Ride Different – Explore NZ’s Premier Range of Electric Dirt Bikes and More Today

name: Butler Marine
description:
As dedicated boat people ourselves, we have a personal interest in ensuring that all our clients enjoy their marine pursuits to the utmost, safely and with reliable equipment. With a full-time staff of five people, we bring the enthusiasm, knowledge and service to meet the needs of clients and would-be clients, to exceed expectations.

 

Our mission is to have satisfied customers, that want to keep coming back to us.

 

JOHN BUTLER, PROPRIETOR

 

I have worked extensively within the marine industry virtually all of my life. My father started Butler Marine in 1970, and I restarted the company name in 1987. I have been involved in the designing and building of boats, both aluminium and fibreglass over many years. My experience with marine propulsion (outboard, stern-drive, and jet power) enables me to comment and advise on all of these systems. I enjoy most forms of fishing which I carry out at Kaikoura, Motunau and the outer reaches of the Marlborough Sounds & Cook Strait. You can contact me at work anytime to discuss your boating needs.

 

BRETT BUTLER, SERVICE MANAGER

 

Brett has been a keen boatie/fisherman all his life. He started working here after school, helping out from age 15, then working with us during our busy summer months while still working full time elsewhere. From age 21 he moved to Perth where he met and married Chrissy – two daughters followed. He moved back to Christchurch in 2015 – straight back into working at Butler Marine. We are lucky to get Brett back as he brings valuable IT skills and excels with electronics.

name: Canterbury Land Rover Owners Club
description:
You might like to know a little more about CLROC. In spite of its name, it is much more than a one make 4WD club - in fact, membership is open to all makes of four-wheel-drive vehicles with capabilities similar to the Land Rover. We count a large number of Toyota Landcruisers and Hiluxes, Nissan Patrols and other similar vehicles amongst our members as well as Land Rovers, Range Rovers and Discoverys.

 

Membership currently stands at between 120 and 130 with typically around 40 people attending our monthly meetings. Meetings are held on the 3rd Tuesday of each month (except January, which is on the 4th Tuesday) at Robbies Bar and Restaurant - 87 Riccarton road (entrance and parking from Nelson st) and run from 7:30pm to about 9:30pm.

 

Our meetings are a chance for members to get together over a drink and a bite to eat. We briefly report back on what the committee has been working on but the general emphasis is on reporting on recent and forthcoming club trips and activity. We may also have slides, videos or a guest speaker.

 

We publish a monthly digital magazine which we hope is entertaining and informative and this gives details about forthcoming trips and social events. It carries some advertising of 4WD relevant products and we encourage our members to support the advertisers as they support us.

name: CJM'S Events Ltd
description:
Welcome to CJM'S Events Ltd – Your Partner in Unforgettable Experiences

 

CJM'S Events Ltd is not just an event management company; we are your gateway to unforgettable experiences. With over 18 years of dedicated service, we bring a wealth of knowledge and expertise to the world of event management.

 

Our Passion for Community and Giving Back

 

At CJM'S Events, we believe in the power of community. That's why we proudly support our local communities by fundraising and donating to various charitable causes, schools, fire brigades, and community groups. In the past year alone, we've contributed over $50,000 to charities like the Heart Foundation of New Zealand and Ronald McDonald House South Island. Over the last 18 years, our donations have surpassed $200,000, and we remain committed to making a positive impact on our community.

 

A Diverse Event Portfolio

 

With a calendar boasting up to 25 actual events per annum and two thriving virtual running event platforms, CJM'S Events is a hub of activity both in New Zealand and Australia. Over the last year, we've hosted more than 100 virtual events, allowing participants to engage in our challenges from around the world. Our team has orchestrated well over 200 actual events, ensuring each one is a seamless and unforgettable experience.

 

Exceptional Experiences for All

 

At CJM'S Events, we're dedicated to providing exceptional experiences for all participants. Whether you're a first-timer or an elite athlete, our events are designed to cater to your needs. We take pride in the seamless execution of our events, bringing unparalleled knowledge and expertise to each one.

 

A Team with Real Experience

 

Our team's experience comes from personal participation in countless events. From local club running, swimming, and cycling events to national track competitions in our younger years, we've been there. We've also tackled Ironman New Zealand and numerous cycling endurance events. This hands-on experience extends to directorship roles in the professional sports arena and executive management of a listed company. You can trust that our team has the knowledge and experience to make your event a resounding success.

 

Join Us for Unforgettable Memories

 

If you're on the lookout for an event that will create lasting memories, look no further than CJM'S Events Ltd. Join us on this extraordinary journey of health, fitness, and community. To learn more about our events or get involved, please don't hesitate to contact us. Reach out to John at john@cjmsevents.co.nz or Carol at carol@cjmsevents.co.nz, or simply give us a call at 027 226 7769. We can't wait to see you at our next event. Let's create unforgettable memories together!

name: Coastguard Tautiaki Moana
description:
Coastguard Tautiaki Moana - We’re for boaties, whatever your waka.

 

Who we are 

 

Coastguard Tautiaki Moana is Aotearoa New Zealand’s primary maritime search and rescue organisation, powered by volunteers, and enabled by staff. Our vision is to help everyone get the best out of their time on the water, safely and with confidence. 

 

Last year our 2,000+ volunteers gave their time to help bring thousands of people home safely to dry land - from Houhora to Bluff and across the oceans, major rivers, and lakes of our beautiful country. Whatever their waka of choice, be it a boat, jet ski, or kayak, we’re side by side with all water-users, no matter the situation.  

 

Our volunteers are based at 62 Coastguard units and communities around the country:  

 	59 units crewing 101 rescue vessels and rescue craft (jet ski) providing services to water-users, positioned in strategic locations around the coast and on major lakes and rivers, from Houhora in the north to Bluff in the south. 	2 air patrol units based in Auckland and Northland who pilot two search and rescue aircraft providing our eyes in the sky. 	A dedicated Operations Centre in Auckland where highly trained volunteers and staff lead search and rescue incidents and routine breakdowns as well as take trip reports, broadcast weather conditions, and monitor various VHF channels across New Zealand.   

We closely collaborate with government-led search and rescue services like the Police and the Rescue Co-ordination Centre (RCCNZ). Together, we ensure a joint effort to coordinate major marine rescues, working hand in hand to keep everyone safe on the water.  

 

When we’re not helping on the water, we’re helping off it, providing the know-how to keep people safe and informed. Our Education and Community Engagement team are the leading providers of boating courses throughout Aotearoa, offering courses to suit all abilities, water-users, and training pathways. 

 

As water safety advocates, we’re committed to reducing preventable watercraft drownings. We do this by sharing all we know through education, community engagement, awareness campaigns, and by teaming up with like-minded partners across the water safety sector.  

 

None of this would be possible without the financial support we receive from our funders, partners, members, and donors who generously give to Coastguard so that our volunteers can continue to be there when New Zealanders need them most.  

 

We’re an organisation that has never stood still. With over two million keen water-users (and growing) out there, we’re committed to being more approachable, friendly, and more relevant to all New Zealanders, regardless of their background or experience on the water. Click here to learn more about our journey and focus for the future.

name: Cycleways
description:
Our Journey

 

Cycleways was founded by Matt Bailey in 2006. Growing up in rural Australia, Matt’s early experiences riding with friends and working in a bike shop ignited a lifelong passion for cycling. When he moved to Ōtautahi, he noticed a gap in service-oriented bike shops and decided to make a difference.

 

Thus, Cycleways was born. What began as a modest workshop with a small retail section, Matt put in the hard yakka - fixing bikes into the wee hours, solving issues, and helping customers find their perfect bike. Today, Cycleways has grown, with a larger team and a broader range of offerings, but our commitment to exceptional service remains as strong as ever.

 

Why Service Matters

 		Without service, it’s just a transaction.

At Cycleways, we care about every customer. Whether you’re a pro rider, weekend warrior or mum or dad looking for a decent kids’ bike, we ask questions, listen to your answers, and offer genuinely helpful advice. We want you to ride out of here stoked with your purchase and confident it’s the right choice for you.	 		A well-serviced bike will protect you.

Bikes are increasingly complicated beasts. From suspension and brakes to electrical issues, if things go wrong on the road or trail, the outcome can be gnarly. Our trained mechanics are experts at solving issues and fine tuning bikes to achieve optimum performance. We care for every bike that comes in as if we were the ones riding it out the door.	 		Better service means better quality too.

Everything we sell at Cycleways we believe in, it’s as simple as that. Because we hand pick and personally test all our brands and products, we wholeheartedly stand by everything we sell and offer outstanding aftersales support too. For amazing bikes and the best gear, come see what we have in store and online.

name: eBikezNZ
description:
eBikezNZ Mobility Solutions

 

We are a growing business, operating by appointment & looking forward to helping you into your next set of wheels, our objective is to offer everyone Personalised Mobility Solutions. Primarily specializing in 3 & 4 wheel mobility e-scooters, if there is a particular style you are after that we do not have, give us a call & we will see what we can do!

In 2009 my wife was diagnosed with MS – Multiple Sclerosis, Primary Progressive and one of the things we found out about MS is, that it adversly affects your mobility. Challenged mobility however does NOT need to define you! We are a bit of an out doorsie kinda family & I wanted to be able to assist her in being able to keep mobile. After a heap of reading, conversations, trials & checks, I began importing scooters, to bridge the gap between being able to be independent – and not..

In 2012 the very 1st model scooter we comenced with was Big Red, since then our range has increased exponentially, where we now carry a variety of over 20 different models. This range aids us to assist in providing you with scooters for everyday use, car replacement styles, to a great variety of travelling and portable companion mobility scooters

Conveniently located in both Christchurch & Rolleston, with a partnership to freight our scooters nationwide – no matter where you are, we’re here for you. We love being able to match the preferred scooter to its new owner, knowing the freedom and independence that it brings. We also have available more than just your typical mobility scooter, we specialise in customising, adapting & personalising your scooter to suit your needs. An example is our Triclops & Harley Boys & expanding into a number of 2-wheel style e-scooters – feel free to come and take them or any of our other scooters for a spin.

We have demo scooters of all models in stock for you to try and available for immediate purchase.

If you wish for something different, we are here to assist you with a personalised service to order in the specific model you request based on your selection & specifications.
Come and see us & find your match!

Cheers

Jamie – eBikezNZ Mobility Solutions

name: GCH Aviation
description:
Garden City Helicopters was established 1983 by John Currie flying from New Brighton Beach, Christchurch City. When the company first started, John was mainly flying scenic tourism flights. As the only helicopter company operating in Christchurch at the time, John was often called on to help emergency services, so the Rescue Helicopter operation became a necessity for the community and started serving the community in 1985.

 

In 1995 following the Cave Creek disaster John introduced the NZ Flying Doctor Service to transport critically ill patients to base hospitals around the country. The expansion of tourism opportunities in Christchurch increased the demand for scenic flights, and the formalisation of a dedicated and expanded 24-hour air rescue operation resulted in a 2018 relocation to a purpose-built base adjacent to Christchurch International Airport. Garden City Helicopters is still widely recognised as one of New Zealand’s premier helicopter operators, servicing all aspects of tourism and charter flights, air ambulance and rescue operations, commercial flying and pilot training.

 

The addition of an executive Jet Centre and the acquisition of a private charter business jet required a new brand for the group of companies so and GCH Aviation Group brand became our new brand. In addition to the Christchurch headquarters, the GCH Aviation Group has helicopter bases in Kaikoura, Greymouth, Nelson, Wellington, and Fiji.

name: Haines Hunter
description:
Designed and built in New Zealand, Haines Hunter boats are known for one thing above all, a feature you can actually feel when you’re out on the water. The legendary Haines Hunter ride!

 

Inspired design, breakthrough innovation, unmatched craftsmanship. In a Haines Hunter, the ride just gets better!

 

75 years and counting

 

They say you can’t buy experience but every Haines Hunter comes with more than 75 years of refined design, construction and testing built-in. Our craftsmen have passed on their skills from one generation to the next, embraced innovation along the way and cherry-picked the best new technologies. Of course, it also means we’ve received decades of customer feedback, which is another reason why the ride just gets better!

 

Built to Last

 

There’s a good reason why you see so many seasoned Haines Hunters out and about. They’re built tough! In fact, Haines has a significantly higher fibre-to-resin ratio in its hull construction than some competitors - adding strength and reducing weight. What’s more, transoms, a critical area of any boat’s construction, are built to eliminate flexing under load – very reassuring when you’re offshore in challenging conditions.

 

Optimal use of materials

 

On board a Haines Hunter, everything you see, as well as everything you don’t see, has been selected for its high quality and performance. For example, decks and bulkheads use a foam core sandwiched between an inner and outer skin. This technique is used in high performance racing boats and reduces weight compared to other construction methods – improving fuel economy without compromising on strength and durability.

 

Deep V Hull Design

 

A precise 21°, Haines Hunter’s Deep V provides the smoothest possible ride because the hull knifes cleanly through waves rather than pounding on them. Furthermore, the hull has longitudinal “strakes” providing lift and throwing spray away from the boat, keeping the deck dry. All in all, the Deep V makes a huge difference to the comfort of all on board.

 

Handlaid GRP Construction

 

Hull construction is a highly skilled craft that demands attention to detail. That’s why this integral structural component of a Haines Hunter is made from GRP, Glass Reinforced Plastic, and laid by hand. It’s very time-consuming process but known to result in superior strength, rigidity and longevity. Until a better construction method comes along, our craftsmen will continue to ply their trade in this time-honoured fashion. At Haines Hunter, there are no shortcuts.

 

Made in New Zealand

 

As well as being manufactured in New Zealand, many fittings and features aboard a Haines Hunter are also sourced here. Stainless rails, rod holders and fuel tanks are all manufactured by trusted local suppliers as is the durable, comfortable foam cushioning beneath the seating. However, if we can’t find it here, we’ll go to the end of the earth in search of quality. Switches from the USA, curved glass from Ireland, we source the best wherever it is!

We have gentle horses, we match the horse to the rider and your level of riding experience and skill.

 

Novice rider and a bit anxious about jumping on a horseback ? Don't worry, our guides are here to make sure that you will be safe all along. name: Huntsman Boats
description:

Designed to stand the test of time, a Huntsman isn’t just a boat — it’s something your family will treasure for years to come.

 

Our Story: Huntsman Boats

 

Huntsman Boats was founded in 1992 and has been proudly New Zealand made ever since. We began with the legendary Stingray ski boats and specialised fibreglass repairs, before expanding into popular early models such as the 525 Executive, 525 Sport, and La Plata. The strong demand for these boats led to full-time production of fibreglass powerboats.

The 525 Executive and Sport laid the foundation for the high-performance hull designs we’ve developed over the years. Today, our range spans from 5 to 7.5 metres, offering something for every kind of boating enthusiast. Across the lineup, Huntsman boats are known for their superior handling, outstanding performance, and meticulous attention to design.

 

Huntsman FIBREGLASS

 

In 2012, we launched Huntsman Fibreglass — our dedicated division for high-quality commercial fibreglass products. From automotive and architectural components to signage, equestrian and farming solutions, we deliver custom fibreglass work trusted across industries.

If you’re looking for expert fibreglass manufacturing outside of boating, get in touch today to see what Huntsman Commercial can do for you.

name: Hybrid Bikes
description:
Hybrid Bikes was born from a deeply personal quest—to regain fitness following a battle with cancer (which I won). From this initial design emerged a bold vision: to establish the premier carbon fibre E-Bike company in Nelson adorned with the iconic New Zealand Fern and to elevate this Kiwi brand onto the global stage.

 

In 2018, our dedication to excellence was validated with the prestigious Fernmark Award, marking the start of our journey. Since then, we have led the field in carbon fibre E-Bike innovation, earning global recognition. In 2023, we received the Global Award for “Most Innovative Leader – E-Bike,” followed by another win in 2024 for our revolutionary “Full Suspension, Step-Through Carbon Fibre E-Bike.” Despite our innovation, financial constraints prevented us from scaling fast enough to compete with billion-dollar brands.

 

Our track record includes the Supreme Business Award in 2021, Deloitte Fast 50 in 2022, and recognition as a High Growth Company in the Asia-Pacific region in 2024. In July 2024, we will proudly represent New Zealand at the Euro Bike Show in Frankfurt, a testament to our leadership in carbon fibre E-Bike design. Hybrid Bike can offer a full range of customized carbon fibre E-Bike to suit everybody. As a micro-sized business, our accomplishments are extraordinary.

name: Jenny's Kitchen Tamarind
description:
Our Tamarind Chutney is Gluten-Free and made with all natural ingredients (Tamarind, Sugar, Spices and Garlic). Jenny's Kitchen Tamarind Chutney was first created on Waiheke Island in 1998. These truly gourmet condiments are still made the old-fashioned way in small batches. Each pot is hand stirred, each jar is hand poured. Jenny's Tamarind Chutney is a true anglo-indian chutney with an exquisite depth of flavour. It's sweetness balances the natural sourness of Tamarind*.

 

There is none of the vinegar taste that dominates so many chutneys, and it is beautifully spiced to create a full flavour with various options of 'hotness' to suit. Choose between Mild, Medium, Hot and Extra Hot. Even in the Extra Hot, the heat does not dominate the flavour, but comes through later. It is fabulous with cheeses, the star turn of any sandwich, and wonderful alongside any meats or poultry.

 

Serve it up over cream cheese as a dip, with poppadoms as part of an indian meal, or use it as a base or topping sauce on a pizza. Jenny's Tamarind Chutney is addictive, and has developed a cult following with foodies who will track it down from the other ends of the earth.

name: Kiwi Spirit Distillery
description:
At Kiwi Spirit Distillery, every bottle tells a story of rich heritage and our relentless pursuit of excellence. Our spirits are crafted with the utmost care, reflecting our dedication to both tradition and innovation.

 

In the heart of Golden Bay, one of New Zealand's most picturesque locales, Kiwi Spirit Distillery embodies a legacy of passion and craftsmanship. As a family owned and operated distillery, our commitment to excellence is unwavering—we prioritize quality over quantity in every handcrafted spirit we produce.

 

Our journey begins amidst the natural splendor of Golden Bay, where creativity thrives alongside our commitment to local sourcing. Ingredients are sourced as local as possible, some even cultivated in the gardens and orchards of our expansive 6-acre distillery and cellar door. This dedication to locality fuels our creativity, allowing us to push boundaries and craft spirits that delight our customers.

 

At Kiwi Spirit Distillery, tradition meets innovation. We honor time-honored techniques while embracing new ideas, resulting in a distinctive range of spirits that showcase the best of New Zealand. Each spirit is meticulously distilled and bottled by hand, infused with the terroir of our surroundings and a touch of Kiwi ingenuity.

 

Join us on a journey from Garden to Glass, where good taste is not just a pleasure—it's a soulful experience.

description:
Experience outdoor comfort like never before with the D-Luxe Rooftop Tent. Designed with innovation in mind, it features a powerful 2-way ventilation fan with three adjustable speeds, underfloor vents, and easy access to power and lights, ensuring you stay comfortable no matter the conditions. Enjoy restful nights on the 70mm dual-layer mattress, including high-density foam and memory foam for superior comfort. Built to withstand the toughest conditions, the D-Luxe comes equipped with a 600N gas strut, a 2.3m ladder for easy access, and a range of practical features like removable side awnings, internal storage pockets, and a lockable CNC latch system for added security. With a 5-year warranty, the D-Luxe is a product you can trust to perform season after season. Optional extras like thermal lining, solar kits, and an annex allow you to tailor your experience even further, ensuring your rooftop adventure is always ready for the next level.

 

At Lucas Creek, we go beyond selling outdoor gear—we're dedicated to enhancing your experience every step of the way. We commit to ensuring you're happy at every step, from the moment you buy to supporting you long after the sale. With a strong attention to detail and custom-designed technology, our products set the standard for quality and innovation. We stand by our rigorous testing process and in-house workshop, ensuring that every item meets our high standards before reaching your hands. At Lucas Creek, you can trust us to deliver exceptional products and unmatched service, keeping you safe, comfortable, and ready for adventure all across New Zealand.

name: Luno - Dawnlight
description:
Luno is the #1 brand for vehicle-based camping. From weekend getaways to epic road trips, we create smart, comfy gear designed to help you camp better and explore more.

 

At Luno, we believe that camping should be easy, comfortable, safe, and accessible. We’re on a mission to get more people outside, comfortably, through thoughtfully designed vehicle-based camping gear.

 

We’re a team of engineers and creatives based in Bend, OR.

At Luno, work and play tend to be the same thing. We love an early-morning surf as much as we love late nights dreaming up new ideas. As product designers and everyday adventurers, we’re driven to create better experiences for people in the outdoors. We’re based in Bend — an incredible hub for car camping.

 

Our gear enables you to camp comfortably out of your rig.

So, after one too many restless, cold, and uncomfortable nights sleeping in the back of our SUVs, we decided it was time for a change. We set out to solve the car-camping problem by focusing on user-oriented solutions, precision design, and durable materials for above-average abuse.

 

Each year, we commit to donating 3% of profits with the goal of supporting environmental stewardship and access to the outdoors.

 

To be a radically responsible business that keeps the betterment of people and planet as our driving force.

name: Lysaght Ltd

Maritime NZ exercises Kaitiakitanga, or guardianship that supports Aotearoa oceans and waterways to be safe, secure and clean. We’re on a multi-year journey working towards becoming an increasingly successful regulator.

name: Morrison Motor Group
description:
The Morrison name has been entrenched in the sales and service sector of Canterbury for over 48 years.

It dates back to the original family business of McKenzie Saddlery and Canvas which had a large customer base throughout mid-Canterbury and the South Island. The values and ethos of their family business was to offer strong customer service, a friendly environment and foster repeat and referral clients.

Fast forward to today and the example of these values are still followed and have led to the success of the Morrison brand. With over 60% of customers coming as either repeats or referrals, it is easy to say that these long-held family values have helped the business grow to where it is today.

name: Mountain High Clothing
description: Mountain High Clothing is a clothing company based in Ashburton, New Zealand. They offer a wide range of outdoor clothing and accessories for men, women, and children. Their products are designed to withstand the rugged conditions of the mountains and provide comfort and protection to outdoor enthusiasts. In addition to clothing, Mountain High Clothing also provides customization services, allowing customers to personalize their garments with logos or designs. With their high-quality products and personalized service, Mountain High Clothing is a trusted choice for those who love outdoor adventures.

name: Mountain Horses Outfitters
description:
Discovering the Spirit of the High Country

 

When the road ends, the real story begins. Out here in New Zealand’s high country, fences fall away and the land rolls wild and open under the Southern sky. This is Mountain Horses Outfitters — your gateway to 78,000 hectares of untamed alpine wilderness in the legendary St James Conservation Area, a stone’s throw from Hanmer Springs.

We’re for the overseas drifters, freedom chasers, and curious souls who crave more than postcards and paved tracks. We saddle up for alpine ridgelines, river crossings, mountain passes, and campfire nights under a cathedral of stars. We ride where rivers carve valleys, where wild deer slip through the tussock at dawn, where steam rises off natural hot pools after a long day in the saddle.

 

What’s waiting for you?
Half-day rambles through alpine meadows. Full-day high country runs. Multi-day packhorse expeditions that cross rivers and crest ridgelines. Nights spent soaking bones in remote natural hot pools, swapping stories by the campfire, dreaming up tomorrow’s trail under a sky so wide you’ll forget your phone even exists.

 

Why ride with us?
Because life’s too short to stand behind fences. Because the best views aren’t found from the highway. Because freedom still exists — and it runs on four hooves, heads high, into the mountains.
So when you’re ready to trade city lights for campfire sparks, and paved roads for river crossings — saddle up. 

 

Your next frontier is waiting.
Book your saddle. Claim your freedom.

name: Mt Fyffe Distillery
description:
Mt Fyffe Distillery: Small batch artisan gin distillery at Kaikōura

 

Mt Fyffe Distillery – our story

 

Hello, my name is Justine and welcome to my small batch artisan distillery. I have always loved gin and a few years back discovered the wonderful world of botanical gin. I live on a sheep farm on the slopes of Mt Fyffe in Kaikōura, one of the most stunning places in New Zealand where the mountains meet the sea.  I realised how many interesting botanics surrounded me and the potential for creating my own unique botanical gins. 

 

Mid 2019 I took a gin making course and the journey began. I started foraging for botanics and playing with blends and flavours with my shiny new 2.5 litre Portuguese copper still. The learning curve was steep, and despite a few hiccups along the way which only made me more determined, I’m very proud to have developed two unique Kaikōura gin’s.

 

My gin blends have been influenced by the stories that surround them. I’m completely absorbed by the entire process from foraging for the botanics, creating each small batch gin with its own bespoke uniqueness through to hand bottling, labelling and creating each gift box. You can be assured of my personal attention to detail at every stage.

 

I spend my time juggling being a farmer’s wife, a mother of two young boys, creating gin in my wee distillery and running or biking around the hills looking for potential botanics!  

 

Life is full of challenges, I thrive on them and after having my children I started looking at ways to diversify and utilise my time on the family farm so I set myself a goal of following a dream I’d had for a long time… to make good, really good gin.

 

With a midwifery background and married to a sheep farmer with two young boys, I knew nothing about what distillation required so happily booked into a gin course in Auckland.  I went thinking I’d quite like it. I didn’t like it, I LOVED it.  I found I had a passion and a talent for working with botanics. That was in mid 2019 and since then I’ve been busy creating, absorbing information to learn the trade and negotiating all the obstacles of setting up a new business.

 

To create a really good gin, you have to start with using a high ethanol percentage, the higher the ABV (alcohol by volume) you start with the smoother the gin.  I opted to import my ethanol, using a grain based ethanol from Southern grain spirits in Kaiapoi at 96.5% ABV.  This ensures I start with a fabulous foundation on which to distill and infuse my botanics with.

 

Read the Full Story

name: Nelson Naturally
description:
Nelson Naturally: From our family, to your table.

 

When my father, Nick Morris, first started making mustard in our family laundry in Nelson back in 1990, neither he nor I could have ever imagined how far that first batch would take us. 

 

Inspired by flavours he’d enjoyed while living overseas and frustrated by the lack of options in NZ stores, Dad set out to create and offer something different to local tables. He always loved the buzz of the back then humble Saturday Market, where he could share his creations. What began as a side-hobby to bring tastes from around the world to local tables, has now grown into a family-owned and operated business spanning three generations.

Over 30 years on - fast forward to today - and we are continuing with the same passion and care that Dad poured into that very first batch. Still a family business at heart, our journey is as much about people as it is about great food. Staying true to our roots by sourcing top-quality ingredients, following time-honored methods, and creating flavors that bring people together. 

From our family to yours, every jar tells a story of where it all started—and where we’re heading next, and we will continue Nick’s legacy to bring you our premium condiments for generations to come.

name: Next72Hours
description:
Next72Hours Disaster Preparedness Supplies and Emergency Food Rations

 

At Next72Hours, our name reflects the critical window of preparedness following a major natural disaster. When such events occur, essential utilities and services may be disrupted or overwhelmed. Emergency responders prioritize the most critical cases, and in some instances, they may be unable to reach affected areas due to damaged infrastructure. It's why being self-sufficient for at least 72 hours, as recommended by numerous agencies, is crucial.

With over 60 years of combined experience in emergency management, disaster response, and victim identification, our team is committed to providing reliable gear precisely when you need it. Based in Ōtautahi, Christchurch City, on New Zealand's South Island, we understand the country's vulnerability to natural disasters. Situated within the Pacific Ring of Fire, New Zealand frequently encounters seismic and volcanic activity, along with unpredictable weather-related disasters.

Despite these risks, many New Zealanders remain unprepared. At Next72Hours, we strive to offer top-quality products, either individually or in premade kits, sourced from the market. We encourage individuals to explore their area's specific hazards and create disaster preparedness checklists through resources like the National Emergency Management Agency (NEMA) website.

We welcome suggestions for product additions and are open to reviewing and testing items for suitability on our platform. Your safety and preparedness are our priorities.

name: NZ Mounted Games Association
description:
Mounted Games is a fast paced, action packed discipline with riders and mounts displaying an incredible amount of fitness, training and skill.Riders must be athletic and strong with wonderful horsemanship skills and great hand to eye coordination.Ponies are required to be nimble, fast, strong and brave but most of all must have a great brain!This exciting equestrian discipline can be enjoyed by everyone and spectators do not require specialist "horsey" knowledge - just a desire to be entertained!In 2013 New Zealand hosted the NRM World Team Championships which was hugely successful and a spectacle never before seen in New Zealand!!There is no doubt the success of the competition was a wonderful testament to the spirit and enthusiasm of the mounted games community in New Zealand and overseas.

 

NZMGA is an affiliate of the International Mounted Games Organisation, of which there are 20 member countries from all over the world.

 

Within NZMGA there are currently branches set up in Northland, Auckland, Waikato,Turangi, Hawkes Bay, Taranaki, Manawatu, Canterbury, Wellington, Western Bay of Plenty and Otago with others pending. 

 

Every year sees National Championships run in Individual, Pairs and Teams competitions. We also run an annual training camp in January, and each branch organises smaller competitions and coaching clinics throughout the year.

 

Competitions are run in age groups ranging from the Under 12 year old "minis" to the veterans at 35 years and over. And some of them are well over! The Open section is available to competitors of any age and is keenly contested each time.

Sandflies hate it,
you’ll love it.

 

Our sandfly repellent is a natural, effective and affordable repellent of New Zealand sandflies and other biting insects. It is DEET-free, non-toxic and safe for use on children.

Many people came back praising it’s effectiveness, and repeated requests led us to try bottling and selling our repellent. That was about 15 years ago.

 

We now sell to numerous retailers throughout New Zealand, and at Department of Conservation Visitor Centres nationwide.

name: Packgeargo
description:
Packgeargo came about in 2016 after being disappointed with what the big  stores could offer us in terms of Ultralight Gear. Whether its age, health or just gram counting people want to get their pack weight down and Packgeargo is New Zealand's leading Ultralight Outdoor Gear Specialists. We are constantly adding and looking for exciting new innovative products for our store to keep it fresh and up to date.

 

Warren and Lucy both love the outdoors, tramping, camping, kayaking, bikepacking when they can. With Lucy working through the week as a Paedeatric Physio the store is run by Warren.

 

Add to that three girl’s and they are a pretty busy family but are always just a call away should you want to chat about a piece of gear or come and try on a pack.

Based in the beautiful Nelson/Tasman area with some amazing nature right on our doorstep we want to get you and your family to enjoy the sheer pleasure and wonderment that comes from being out there, enjoying the natural environment and not having to worry about your gear.

 

Check out the fantastic range of gear we have on offer, read about the product, see customer reviews. If after that your still not sure, the give us a ring, email or book a time to come and see us. We are more than happy to answer your questions and find the solution that is just right for you.

name: Packrafting Queenstown
description:
We specialise in small group guided packrafting trips and courses from our base in Queenstown New Zealand. Queenstown is known as the adventure capital of New Zealand and offers some of the most beautiful and pristine environments to explore. Packrafts – lightweight, packable boats, have changed the way we travel through the backcountry.

 

Weighing only 3kg (6 pounds) and packing down to the size of a sleeping bag means these boats can easily be carried into previous inaccessible and untouched rivers. Despite their small size and weight, Packrafts are incredibly tough, easy to paddle and capable of handling some challenging whitewater. Our trips are designed to take full advantage of this as we hike, bike and paddle through the most gorgeous packing scenery in New Zealand.

name: Perfect Pitched
description:
The Perfect Pitched concept has grown with the goal to encourage others to get out and about, enjoying the countryside and everything the beautiful outdoors of NZ has to offer, but also still being cost efficient and budget-friendly.

 

Being a family owned NZ company, the brand "Perfect Pitched" is our own and via direct import, no overseas shareholders, no other middle men, no huge overheads -- we work from home-- it means we can offer a high quality product range (feel free to compare the specs with other RTT brands) at affordable levels! Perfect Pitched is the brainchild of Peter and Pia. Peter and Pia have always enjoyed exploring the outdoors; 4WD trips, hunting, fishing and tramping.

 

However, by the time they had five children, they began to discover that camping could be a costly and stressful experience. Undaunted by the difficulties of traditional camping, the Kouwenberg family researched for alternative options for camping. After coming across rooftop tents and awnings, they felt they had found the perfect solution for camping without stress or fuss.

 

They tried this idea extensively overseas with their children and found their enjoyment in family camping restored again. At the end of the day, sitting around a crackling fire, a stick in hand and bag of marshmallows. Nothing ever tasted as good as a melted marshmallow cooked in the embers of the fire. Add your fav beverage...life's good!

 

Evenings in the campsite shared with friends or family and a few good stories, this is what memories are made of. And after the fire dies down, everyone heads into their Perfect Pitched roof top tent for the night. No matter if you're a solo camper of a family of 5; camp for work or leisure...There’s a Perfect Pitched camping option for you. And maybe one for your dog, too.

description:

An engineer and experienced outdoorsman, Chris Harrington, combined the two passions in his life; adventuring and creating. This combination sparked the development of the Quest Bike Trailer back in 2010. name: R.M.Williams
description:
In 1932 our founder, Reginald Murray Williams AO, CMG began to make bush saddlery and boots for sale, while camped at Nepabunna, in South Australia's remote Gammon Ranges. Here R.M. first met 'Dollar Mick Smith', an itinerant saddler. The two men after much trial and error were able to fashion a pair of stockman's riding boots.

 

Over 85 years on, R.M. Williams continues to make world renowned boots, accessories and men's and women's apparel that are purposefully designed and sustainable to enjoy on life's adventures. Today, R.M.Williams has 65 retail stores in Australia, 2 stores in New Zealand, 3 stores in the USA and 3 stores in the UK as well as over 900 stockists around the globe.

 

R.M.Williams is a global company, yet will always remain passionate about the Australian bush. In 2020 Tattarang acquired 100% of R.M.Williams from L Catterton bringing the brand back into Australian hands for the first time since 2014. Tattarang is one of Australia's largest private investment groups owned by Andrew and Nicola Forrest. The investment of the Forrest family will ensure the Australian craftsmanship of R.M.Williams continues to be loved and worn all around the world.

name: Remarkable Cream
description:
Remarkable Cream's authentic cream liqueur recipe has been developed by three generations of Kiwis. We proudly use New Zealand Whey Vodka as the base spirit. Here is why. New Zealand whey vodka is a type of vodka that is made from whey, which is a byproduct of milk, cheese, and protein powder production. Whey is the liquid remaining after milk has been turned to curd and strained. It contains proteins, minerals, and importantly - lactose.

 

In the production of New Zealand whey vodka, the whey undergoes an innovative concentration process before beginning its fermentation process, where yeast is added to convert the sugars present in the whey into alcohol. After fermentation, the liquid is distilled multiple times to achieve a high level of purity and remove impurities. The use of whey as a base for vodka imparts unique characteristics to the final product. Whey vodka is often described as having a smooth and creamy texture with subtle hints of sweetness.

 

Those characteristics of whey vodka compliment well with what we want to achieve in our cream liqueur: smooth and creamy with a clean palate. New Zealand is known for its dairy industry, and the use of whey in vodka production showcases the country's commitment to sustainable practices by utilizing byproducts that would otherwise go to waste. Whey vodka has gained popularity for its innovative approach and its ability to offer a distinct flavor experience compared to traditional grain-based vodkas.

name: Shuzi
description:
Gary Goodfellow — Owner of Shuzi NZ

 

Gary Goodfellow was a professional motorcycle racer for 20 years, ranked 5th and 8th in the world for both Motorcross and Superbike racing respectively. Gary was the first New Zealander to win a World Super Bike race and was the first person to win a World Super Bike race for Suzuki. Gary’s racing career included winning over 11 championships held in Canada and representing North America in the Trans-Atlantic Championships held in England. Gary was also a test pilot for the John Britten development Team in New Zealand for 3 years.

​

Over the course of his professional racing career Gary had numerous injuries from racing incidents. The ongoing affects of these injuries had a huge impact on his quality of life and ability to do every day tasks. Normal activities such as standing and walking were not enjoyable as after a few hours he would have to sit down to get blood flow back to his feet.

 

Gary was always open to alternative pain management therapies, and a decade ago he tried Shuzi for the first time in Canada. The results were amazing! Within a few days he was able to stand and walk without having to sit down and his whole quality of life improved. There was a significant change in energy levels, flexibility and reduction in aches and pains. Gary also noticed a huge reduction in pain killer intake which was a huge benefit for ongoing quality of life.

Chris built his first barrel smoker from a hot water cylinder and became a regular fixture at markets, festivals and events around Canterbury. Since then the company has developed down-to-earth catering menus, and in August 2O18 opened a container restaurant in downtown Christchurch. As the city became less transitional, the container moved on and the team joined forces with Brew Academy for almost three years before opening their very own location in March 2023 at 616 Ferry Road, Woolston.
 
 We love our food, and we know you will too.

name: South Island Overlanders
description:
Overlanding is a unique and exciting way to explore the great outdoors, taking you to places you would never see on a typical vacation. It involves traveling over land, often in a rugged 4x4 vehicle or an off-road capable camper, and spending nights in remote locations. For many, overlanding is a way to escape the hustle and bustle of everyday life, to reconnect with nature, and to challenge oneself in a new and exciting way.

 

South Island Overlanders is aimed at older couples (but all are welcome), overlanding can be an especially rewarding experience. Overlanding provides a unique opportunity to travel at a slower pace, to explore off the beaten path, and to immerse oneself in the beauty of nature. For those who may be feeling a bit burnt out from their daily routines, overlanding can offer a chance to recharge, to disconnect from technology, and to reconnect with each other. However, overlanding can also be challenging, especially for those who are new to the experience. It requires careful planning, preparation, and a willingness to adapt to changing circumstances and by joining a tour, participants can share knowledge and experiences, learn from each other, and build a sense of community. South Island Overlanders focuses on travelling the lesser travelled path and as Tolkien said “not all who wander are lost”

name: The Original Smoke & Spice Co
description:
The Original Smoke and Spice Company was set up by Marcus Peters, a chef with over 20 years of cooking on the international stage. Marcus started smoking large “printanor” garlic bulbs from Marlborough for their high volatile-oil content and strong flavour.

 

They are smoked whole over manuka, bay leaves and rosemary for maximum flavour, while retaining the firmness of the cloves. The resulting garlic is mild, with a slightly nutty, herby flavour along with the expected smokey, woody tones. It adds an extra flavour to many foods. To follow on from the smoked garlic, Marcus developed a smoked garlic seasoning.

 

Pure New Zealand solar sea salt is hand smoked over manuka wood fires, then tumble roasted with pure New Zealand garlic, shallots, corriander, mustard seed and the mighty chilli. The result is outstanding – a seasoning with complexity and depth of flavour considered by chefs and culinaries the world over to be the finest seasoning ever produced. From seafood to red meats, BBQ to vegetarian, this all-purpose culinary seasoning has a unique plateau of flavours that compliment all savoury foods.

 

Our newest addition is our fresh and zesty lemon seasoning. Made from Marlborough solar dried sea salt it is lightly smoked with lemon, fennel, sesame and cracked pepper. The lighter flavour is ideal for white meats and seafood. You can find our products at markets, trade shows and local stockists around New Zealand.

name: Westwind Moto
description:
Westwind Moto: Inspired by the Journey

 

Born from countless motorcycle journeys across northwestern China, Westwind Moto is driven by a passion for adventure riding and a deep understanding of riders' needs. Our mission is to provide simple, practical, reliable, and thoughtfully designed equipment for enthusiasts who are ready to push boundaries and explore uncharted terrain.

The emblem in our logo, the intrepid honey badger, represents the brand’s spirit: Move Forward, Fearlessly.

 

Founded in early 2020 by three motorcycle adventure enthusiasts, Westwind Moto quickly became a trusted name in the motorcycle travel gear industry, earning recognition from riders nationwide. In the same year, we partnered with Dakar Rally racer Zhang Min to establish the Zhang Min Riding Academy, promoting safe and responsible off-road riding. By 2023, we proudly supported the Kove Dakar Motorcycle Team as an official sponsor, contributing to the growth of domestic off-road motorcycling culture.

 

At Westwind Moto, we are committed to delivering practical, reliable products with a focus on user experience, customer feedback, and a management approach centered on transparency, equality, and results.

 

Westwind Moto isn’t just a brand—it’s a commitment to adventure, reliability, and the spirit of fearless exploration.

name: White Pointer Boats
description:
30 years ago White Pointer boats was founded by a visionary boat builder named Rex Briant. Rex had a deep love for the ocean and a burning desire to create boats that could conquer the challenging waters of New Zealand's coastline while providing an unparalleled experience for its occupants.

 

He believed that the perfect vessel could not only withstand the fiercest waves but also offer unmatched comfort and safety to those on board. Driven by this vision, Rex, along with his team of skilled craftsmen, set out to design and build a boat that would set a new standard in the industry. They were determined to create a vessel that would seamlessly blend power, performance, and aesthetics. After countless hours of research, design, and rigorous testing, their dream took shape.

 

In the mid 1990s, the first White Pointer boat was born. It was a boat that combined cutting-edge hull design, superior materials, and meticulous craftsmanship. This boat proved to be a game-changer, catching the attention of serious anglers and adventure seekers alike. Word quickly spread about the White Pointer's exceptional capabilities. Anglers discovered that it could handle the roughest seas, making it an ideal choice for pursuing trophy catches in New Zealand's challenging marine environment.

 

Families realized that it offered a level of safety, comfort, and versatility that was previously unheard of in the world of boating. As the years went by, White Pointer boats continued to evolve and grow in popularity. The brand became synonymous with reliability, innovation, and uncompromising quality. The company expanded its range to include various sizes and configurations, catering to a wide spectrum of marine enthusiasts.

 

Today, White Pointer boats are not just vessels; they are a testament to the enduring spirit of those who dared to dream and create something extraordinary. From their humble beginnings over 30 years ago, they have sailed into the hearts of adventurers, anglers, and families worldwide.

 

The legacy of White Pointer boats is not just about the vessels themselves; it's about the passion, craftsmanship, and dedication that has gone into their creation. It's a story of how a group of individuals, united by their passion for the ocean, reshaped the boating industry, one hull at a time. And as long as there are oceans to explore and adventures to be had, the story of White Pointer boats will continue to unfold on the open waters, leaving a wake of excellence and innovation in its path.

name: Wild Kitchens
description:
Welcome to Wild

 

Proudly kiwi Made every Wild Kitchen is designed and built in our Christchurch workshop, crafted to thrive in New Zealand’s unique conditions, 100% outdoor rated and low maintenance by design. The Dekton, Sintered Stone exterior and Hettich Alfresco hardware give every Wild Kitchen the ability to thrive in the outdoors.

The concept of cooking a BBQ and entertaining outdoors has certainly always been a kiwi backyard thing to do, but installing a functioning kitchen outdoors, that is not so common, and as we found out it’s also not that easy to source. In true Kiwi style if you can’t find what you’re looking for, you build your own. So this is where we began, with a vision and an end goal to cook our sausages in a fabulous alfresco kitchen. We spent over a year researching, testing, and redesigning until we had a kitchen that lived up to our expectations. Built entirely of 100% outdoor rated materials and clad in Sintered Stone this was a kitchen that thrived in the elements. We have grown organically from there, with the simple recipe for creating a product that resonates with kiwis who love to live and entertain outdoors, kiwis who see the value in going Wild.

name: XpeditioNZ
description:
XpeditioNZ brings unparralleled experience to the NZ market. Whether at home in New Zealand, or testing a new route through Mongolia's Gobi Desert, pre-Covid XpeditioNZ conducted fascinating private worldwide 4wd expeditions & overland travel adventures. Over the years, we've been active in dozens of countries on 5 continents.

 

Covid thwarted (for now at least!) our plans to offer 'XpeditioNZ expeditions' commercially, making it possible for small groups (with no 4wd or overland travel experience required) to enjoy an experience of a lifetime, using our fleet of specially prepared 4wd's and following the GPS routes we've spent years plotting, testing and recording across remote and fascinating parts of our planet.
